phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — ablaze combo chains edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your ablaze combo chains beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — ablaze combo chains edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your ablaze combo chains beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your ablaze combo chains in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your ablaze combo chains on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the wall-frost poke control beat your ablaze combo chains cleanly. Trade only off Anivia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: W-poke his last-hits from max range — the pillar chunks early and the lane learns to flinch by level 2.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: E joins: the point-and-click ablaze into Q-stun threat is already a kill pattern with jungle help.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E-Q stun into W is half a health bar at level 3. He respects every cast from here or pays.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your ablaze combo chains beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Your ablaze combo chains out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at the level-6 breakpoint — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean your way — ablaze combo chains edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Brand is a teamfight detonator: spread Blaze, R the clump, and watch three health bars argue with physics.

phases.why: Liandry's spike: the burn makes every rotation percent-health math. Force extended exchanges.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the reflect-barrier poke out-tick your ablaze combo chains in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — ablaze combo chains edge out the clone-root deception when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your ablaze combo chains beat Neeko's clone-root deception on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E-Q stun into W is half a health bar at level 3. He respects every cast from here or pays.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your ablaze combo chains beat Neeko's clone-root deception on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Pyroclasm online — any clumped trade is now a detonation chain. Lane all-ins favor the arsonist.
